channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Police say 11 are killed when a gasoline truck explodes near Uganda's capital
item.description: KAMPALA, Uganda (AP) — At least 11 people were killed Tuesday after a fuel truck exploded next to a highway in Uganda, police said. Two children were among the dead.
item.dc:creator: RODNEY MUHUMUZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Blinken urges Netanyahu to seek truces in Lebanon, Gaza
item.description: Secretary of State Antony J. Blinken met with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Tuesday to discuss ending the conflict in Lebanon and Gaza in addition to securing pathways for humanitarian aid, according to a readout from the meeting.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Iran Revolutionary Guard officer charged in plot to kill American author in US
item.description: The Justice Department unsealed an indictment against four Iranian officials Tuesday for their alleged involvement in a murder-for-hire targeting Iranian-American journalist Masih Alinejad.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Desperation amid search for survivors of an airstrike on a crowded area near major Beirut hospital
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Nearly 16 hours after an Israeli airstrike hit across the street from Beirut’s main public hospital, rescuers were still removing debris Tuesday from the overcrowded slum area.
item.dc:creator: SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Mexico announces food and agriculture plan that could take the country back to the 1980s
item.description: Mexico's new president has announced an agriculture plan that could make the country's food production and distribution look a lot more like it did in the 1980s.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Cuba fights to come back from a nationwide blackout, then a storm that killed six
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — A small town in far eastern Cuba was recovering Tuesday from flooding that killed at least six people after Oscar crossed the island’s eastern coast as a tropical storm with winds and heavy rain.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRIGUEZ and MILEXSY DURAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A UN report says it could take 350 years for Gaza to rebuild if it remains under a blockade
item.description: U.N. agencies have long warned that it could take decades to rebuild Gaza after Israel's offensive against Hamas, one of the deadliest and most destructive military campaigns since World War II. Now, more than a year into the war, a new report speaks in terms of centuries. The U.N.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Woman becomes stuck upside down between boulders for 7 hours while trying to retrieve dropped phone
item.description: A woman who became trapped in between two boulders in Australia after falling into a crevice while trying to retrieve her phone has been saved, NSW Ambulance says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Police say 11 are killed when a gasoline truck explodes near Uganda's capital
item.description: Police in Uganda say least 11 people including children were killed when a fuel truck exploded next to a highway on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Muhumuza, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ukraine blasts UN chief over potential meeting with Putin at BRICS, says stayed away from Kyiv's peace summit
item.description: Ukraine blasted U.N. Secretary-General Guterres for allegedly agreeing to meet with Russian President Putin during the BRICS summit as leaders from the West's top adversaries like China and Iran convene.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Austria's chancellor asked to form new government after parties refuse to work with far-right leader
item.description: VIENNA (AP) — Austria's president on Tuesday tasked incumbent Chancellor Karl Nehammer with forming a new government after all other parties refused to work with the leader of the far-right Freedom Party, which last month won a national election for the first time.
item.dc:creator: PHILIPP JENNE and GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2 transgender women stabbed to death at home in northwest Pakistan
item.description: PESHAWAR, Pakistan (AP) — Two men armed with daggers killed two transgender women at their home in conservative northwest Pakistan overnight before fleeing the scene, police said, a sign of increasing violence against trans people in the country.
item.dc:creator: RIAZ KHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FBI says it's investigating unauthorized release of documents on Israel's possible attack plans
item.description: The FBI says it's investigating the unauthorized release of classified documents on Israel’s preparation for a potential retaliatory attack on Iran.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Navalny's memoir details isolation and suffering in a Russian prison — and how he never lost hope
item.description: In his posthumous memoir “Patriot,” Russian opposition leader Alexei Navalny tracks the boredom, isolation, exhaustion, suffering and absurdity of prison life, while commenting on everything from French literature to Billie Eilish.
item.dc:creator: Hillel Italie And Dasha Litvinova, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says only a US green light on a NATO invitation will sway a reluctant Germany
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine's president is hoping allies will take a more positive stance on his so-called “victory plan” after the U.S. election, but concedes that its key demand — an invitation to join NATO — is not welcome by some major Western partners, in particular Germany.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China holds live-fire drills near Taiwan, in another show of force
item.description: China's military is conducting live-fire drills around islands near Taiwan Tuesday that are being tracked by the Taiwan Defense Ministry.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Samoan coast where King Charles will visit worries about the future after ship damaged reef
item.description: SIUMU, Samoa (AP) — Under clear skies Tuesday, shortly after sunrise in the beachside fishing village of Siumu, Samoa, a dozen men and children prepared their small boats for a day at sea.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY and AYAKA McGILL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gunmen kill 7 people working on a strategic tunnel project in Indian-controlled Kashmir
item.description: SRINAGAR, India (AP) — Gunmen fatally shot at least seven people working on a strategic tunnel project in Indian-controlled Kashmir and injured at least five others, officials said on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea demands withdrawal of North Korean troops allegedly helping Russia fight Ukraine
item.description: South Korea issued a demand to Russia to condemn the cooperation between Russia and North Korea, calling for the withdrawal of North Korean troops from Russia's war with Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Zealand airport imposes cuddle cap with 3-minute limit on goodbye hugs to avoid traffic jams
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — Emotional farewells are a common sight at airports, but travelers leaving the New Zealand city of Dunedin will have to be quick.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Austin warns 'no silver bullet' to defeat Putin as US aid hangs in balance ahead of elections
item.description: Secretary of Defense Austin said there is "no silver bullet" to defeat Russian President Putin and urged the global community to bolster aid to Ukraine as concern mounts over continued U.S. support.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel hammers Hezbollah finances with crushing airstrikes
item.description: Israel targeted branches of a Hezbollah-run financial institution across Lebanon on Sunday, but there are not believed to be any casualties.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel begins strikes against Lebanon targeting Hezbollah financial assets
item.description: The Israeli Defense Forces began conducting airstrikes in Lebanon on Sunday targeting financial institutions linked to the Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Encircling Taiwan was a smokescreen for China's real goal of convincing US not to intervene, expert says
item.description: The Joint Sword exercises used more military hardware and got closer to Taiwan than ever, but some experts say China’s bigger plan is dissuading the U.S. from even attempting to come to Taiwan’s aid.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NHC monitoring Hurricane Oscar as it nears Cuba
item.description: Hurricane Oscar is being closely monitored by the National Hurricane Center as it makes its way toward Cuba on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Netanyahu promises retaliation following assassination attempt by Hezbollah
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu warned Hezbollah that its alleged attempt to assassinate him and his wife was a "grave mistake.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Strike on Netanyahu home 'a signal' Iran proxies not incapacitated, expert says
item.description: A recent drone strike targeting the home of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is sending "a signal" that Iran-backed militant groups are not "incapacitated," despite losing senior leadership in recent weeks, according to a Middle East expert. Daniel Levy, the president of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Harvard donations drop; alums cut ties over anti-Israel protests
item.description: Harvard, the nation's richest university, saw donations plunge 15% after campus protests, new report shows.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, The Hill

item.title: Japan’s ruling party HQ attacked with firebombs; man arrested: Reports
item.description: A man threw several firebombs into the headquarters of Japan’s ruling party in Tokyo Saturday, media outlets report.
item.dc:creator: Yuri Kageyama/The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Cuba’s grid goes offline with massive blackout after a major power plant fails
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — Cuba’s electrical grid went down Friday after one of the island’s major power plants failed, a day after a massive blackout swept across the Caribbean island and with no official estimate for when service will be restored.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRÍGUEZ, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel needs to commit to Gaza rebuilding: Gen. David Petraeus
item.description: A lasting peace will depend on the will of the Israeli coalition government, said the retired Army general and former CIA director.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Israel says Hamas' top leader Yahya Sinwar was killed in Gaza
item.description: Israel’s foreign minister has confirmed that Israeli troops in Gaza have killed Hamas’ top leader Yahya Sinwar, a chief architect of last year's attack on Israel that sparked the war.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa, Samy Magdy, Jack Jeffery, WGN 9

item.title: US long-range B-2 stealth bombers target underground bunkers of Yemen's Houthi rebels
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — The U.S. military unleashed B-2 stealth bombers to target underground bunkers used by Yemen’s Houthi rebels early Thursday, a major escalation in the American response to the rebels' attacks on Mideast shipping lanes that appeared to be a warning to Iran as well.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: North Korea says its revised constitution defines South Korea as 'hostile state' for first time
item.description: South Korea condemned the North's constitutional revision as “an anti-unification, anti-national act.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli strikes kill 27 in Lebanon, including in a town with a dark history of civilian deaths
item.description: QANA, Lebanon (AP) — Israeli airstrikes pounded areas across Lebanon, killing at least 27 people over the past 24 hours, officials said Wednesday, including more than a dozen in a southern town where Israeli bombardments in previous conflicts are seared into local memory.
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD ZAATARI, KAREEM CHEHAYEB, and SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: No slowing of US government contracts for Raytheon amid Qatar bribery charges
item.description: Under the agreement, the company will have to demonstrate good conduct for the next 3½ years and foster a culture of ethics and compliance with anti-corruption laws.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation staff, NewsNation
